title: FDRE Constitution
content: All persons are equal before the law and are entitled without any discrimination to equal protection of the law.

title: FDRE Constitution
content: Everyone has the right to privacy. This right includes protection against interference with personal and family life.

title: Ethiopian Civil Code
content: A valid contract requires agreement between parties, capacity to contract, and a lawful object.

title: Ethiopian Civil Code
content: An obligation is a legal relationship that requires one party to perform an action for another party.

title: FDRE Constitution
content: Accused persons have the right to a public trial and the right to defend themselves before a court.

title: FDRE Constitution
content: Every person has the right to liberty and security. No person shall be deprived of liberty except according to procedures established by law.

title: FDRE Constitution
content: Everyone has the right to bring a justiciable matter before a court or other competent body with judicial power.

title: Ethiopian Civil Code
content: A contract is created when parties reach an agreement that creates legal obligations recognized by law.

title: Ethiopian Criminal Law
content: A person may be held criminally responsible only when the requirements defined by criminal law are fulfilled.

title: FDRE Constitution
content: A person arrested has the right to be informed of the reasons for the arrest and the charges against them.
